hypothesis: For GNN training on datasets whose per-graph node-count distribution has excess kurtosis > 1 and coefficient of variation > 0.75, using dynamic batching (Jraph/TF‑GNN style) will reduce mean combined training time per step by at least 1.5× versus static-2N batching on a single A100 GPU across SchNet, MPEU, and PaiNN models with batch sizes 32–128 while yielding final test RMSEs that are not statistically different (two-sided t-test p > 0.05).
